paper_title: Sedimentary Ancient DNA and Pollen Analysis

methods.description: This method involves extracting and amplifying ancient DNA from sediment samples to identify and analyze the presence of various taxa through sequencing.

steps.description: Sedimentary ancient DNA extractions and polymerase chain reactions (PCRs) followed the same procedures as described in [30].
steps.searchable_summary: perform DNA extraction and PCR on sediment samples

steps.description: Two positive PCR products were pooled for sequencing under the condition that the extraction and PCR negative controls remained negative.
steps.searchable_summary: pool positive PCR products for sequencing

steps.description: Purified all PCR products using the MinElute PCR Purification Kit, following the manufacturer's recommendations.
steps.searchable_summary: purify PCR products using MinElute kit

steps.description: DNA concentrations were estimated with the dsDNA BR Assay and the Qubit 2.0 fluorometer.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate DNA concentration using Qubit fluorometer

steps.description: Purified PCR-products were pooled such that 60 ng DNA of each PCR-product was in the final multiplex.
steps.searchable_summary: pool PCR products to achieve balanced DNA concentration

steps.description: Library preparation and paired-end sequencing were performed by Fasteris SA sequencing service using an Illumina HiSeq 2500 platform.
steps.searchable_summary: perform library preparation and sequencing using Illumina HiSeq

methods.description: This method involves extracting and analyzing pollen from sediment samples to identify and quantify different plant taxa.

steps.description: Pollen extraction of 1 mL sediment was performed following the standard procedure including HCL, KOH, HF (including boiling) and acetolysis.
steps.searchable_summary: extract pollen from sediment using chemical treatment

steps.description: A Lycopodium spore tablet was added to each sample to estimate the pollen concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium spore tablet to estimate pollen concentration

steps.description: Pollen slides were analysed with a Zeiss Axioskop 2 light microscope (400-600× magnification).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen slides using light microscope

steps.description: Taxonomic identification was based on published pollen and non-pollen palynomorph atlases and a pollen reference collection.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen taxa using reference atlases and collections

methods.method_name: Filtering of Illumina Sequencing Data and Taxonomic Assignments
methods.description: This method involves quality checking and filtering Illumina sequencing data, followed by taxonomic assignment using reference databases.

steps.description: The sequence quality was checked using FastQC.
All bioinformatic filtering steps were performed using OBITools.
The taxonomic assignments were performed using two reference databases.
Rare sequences that exhibited less than 10 counts in a sample were excluded from the analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude rare sequences with less than 10 counts

steps.description: Exotic sequences assigned to cultivated plants or those highly unlikely to occur in arctic or boreal landscapes were also excluded.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude exotic sequences unlikely to occur in study area
